source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Slip/Trip/Fall

actions_taken: Safety stand down held with all crews
Swing gate installed and secured with tie offs at access opening as corrective action
H&P Driller evacuated by medevac helicopter to Hospital in Houma, Louisiana
event.summary: At approximately 0030 hours on 14 April 2022, a Helmerich & Payne Driller sustained a back injury with fractures while installing washers and nuts onto bolts on the upper working platform leg of the H&P 406 rig's 18.75-inch BOP on Shell's Auger TLP. The Driller was standing on a lower working platform approximately 3 feet 3 inches above the deck when he stepped off the unprotected side of the platform and fell backwards approximately 3 feet 3 inches, landing on and striking the edge of the BOP skid plate. The incident resulted in a Lost Time Accident greater than 3 days.
key_phrases: JSA and Fall Protection Plan discrepancy

hazards.name: Working at height on elevated platforms
Unprotected platform edges and access openings
Hard surfaces and equipment edges (BOP skid plate)

threats.name: Worker stepping off unprotected platform edge
Loss of situational awareness while working at height
Absence of fall arrest systems during platform work

consequences.name: Lost time accident (>3 days) and medical evacuation

controls.name: Guardrails and swing gates at platform access openings
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent falls from platform edges

supporting_text: no guardrails were installed to prevent falls at the access point opening to the lower working platform
a lower working platform guardrail or swing gate at the access area where the H&P Driller fell was not included in the drawings for that stage of the operation
The BOP work platform being assembled was not equipped with protective handrails at the time of event in this stage of the operation

controls.name: Fall protection gear (body harness with shock absorbing double-leg lanyard)
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to arrest falls

supporting_text: the H&P Driller was not wearing any fall protection gear as recommended in the JSA
H&P's Fall Protection Plan stated that a properly fitting body harness with a shock absorbing double-leg lanyard shall be worn while working at heights greater than 4-feet above the working surface

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for Installing Work Platforms onto BOPs
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to communicate fall protection requirements

supporting_text: H&P's JSA specified that 'Always remain 100% tied off while working at heights' and 'Always use 100% fall protection'
H&P's JSA for Installing Work Platforms onto BOPs that recommended as a safe practice to 'Always use 100% fall protection'; however, it did not address the requirements in H&P's Fall Protection Plan

controls.name: Worker situational awareness and attention to surroundings
controls.barrier_role: Human behavioral control to prevent accidental stepping off platform

supporting_text: the H&P Driller was looking up when standing on the lower platform floor while installing the nuts and bolts onto the upper platform leg and he was not situationally aware that when he repositioned himself, he stepped off the unprotected side of the lower working platform

the H&P Driller's lack of situational awareness when repositioning himself and looking up to secure the upper platform leg caused him to step off and fall backwards from the lower platform's unprotected side

controls.name: Onsite supervision and work observation
controls.barrier_role: Detection and intervention to prevent or mitigate fall consequences

supporting_text: A nearby H&P employee that observed the H&P Driller's fall responded, rendered aid, and escorted him to the Auger TLP medic for medical attention

controls.name: Onshore medical consultation and medevac evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Medical response and emergency evacuation to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: An onshore physician was consulted about the H&P Driller's injury, and it was recommended that he should be sent in for a medical evaluation
At 0836 hours, the H&P Driller was evacuated by a medevac helicopter to a Hospital located in Houma, Louisiana where an onshore physician diagnosed that he had sustained fractures to his back

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
